title: Upon Truggin.
content:
Truggin a footman was; but now, grown lame,
    Truggin now lives but to belie his name.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert Herrick, titled "Upon Truggin.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
